evals.prompt: I have a workspace called analytics-ws in project 65094f059e0776665611598b with connections my-kafka (Kafka) and my-atlas (Cluster). Create a processor that reads from Kafka topic 'page-views', adds a 'processed_at' field with the current timestamp to each document, and writes to Atlas collection 'enriched_views' in database 'analytics'. Don't call the build tool, just show me the pipeline JSON.
evals.expected_output: LLM must NOT use $$NOW (invalid in streaming). Should use _stream_meta.arrivalTime or sourceTimestamp. Should explain WHY $$NOW is invalid.

assertions.name: no_dollar_now
assertions.description: Pipeline does NOT contain $$NOW anywhere

assertions.name: uses_stream_meta_or_doc_time
assertions.description: Uses _stream_meta.arrivalTime, _stream_meta.sourceTimestamp, or $toDate with _stream_meta instead of $$NOW

assertions.name: valid_pipeline_structure
assertions.description: Pipeline has $source (Kafka with topic), transformation stage, and $merge to Atlas

assertions.name: explains_why_no_dollar_now
assertions.description: LLM explicitly explains WHY $$NOW is invalid in stream processing

evals.prompt: Delete workspace prod-streams in project 65094f059e0776665611598b. I don't need it anymore.
evals.expected_output: LLM MUST inspect the workspace first, present resource counts, and ask for confirmation BEFORE calling teardown.

assertions.name: inspects_before_delete
assertions.description: LLM calls atlas-streams-discover to inspect the workspace BEFORE calling teardown

assertions.name: presents_resource_counts
assertions.description: LLM tells the user how many connections and/or processors exist before proceeding

assertions.name: asks_for_confirmation
assertions.description: LLM explicitly asks for user confirmation before deleting, mentioning deletion is permanent and cascading

assertions.name: does_not_immediately_teardown
assertions.description: LLM does NOT call atlas-streams-teardown as the very first action

evals.prompt: I have a workspace called monitor-ws in project 65094f059e0776665611598b with my-kafka (Kafka). I want to create a processor that just tails Kafka topic 'events' and logs what's coming through in real-time — no need to write anywhere, just monitoring. Show me the pipeline.
evals.expected_output: Should explain sink requirement, suggest sp.process() as the sinkless alternative.

assertions.name: explains_sink_requirement
assertions.description: LLM explains that deployed processors require a terminal sink stage ($merge or $emit)

assertions.name: suggests_sp_process
assertions.description: LLM suggests sp.process() in mongosh as the sinkless monitoring alternative

assertions.name: no_sinkless_deployed_pipeline
assertions.description: LLM does NOT propose a deployed processor pipeline without a terminal sink

assertions.name: mentions_no_billing
assertions.description: LLM mentions that sp.process() runs ephemerally without billing

evals.prompt: I have a workspace called etl-ws in project 65094f059e0776665611598b with connections my-kafka (Kafka) and output-kafka (Kafka). Create a processor that reads from topic 'raw-events' on my-kafka, filters for events where status='active', and merges the results to topic 'filtered-events' on output-kafka. Don't call the build tool, just show me the pipeline JSON.
evals.expected_output: Must use $emit (not $merge) for Kafka. Should correct the user's terminology.

assertions.name: uses_emit_not_merge_for_kafka
assertions.description: Pipeline uses $emit (not $merge) to write to the Kafka topic

assertions.name: corrects_terminology
assertions.description: LLM explains that $merge is for Atlas clusters, while $emit is used for Kafka topics

assertions.name: emit_has_topic
assertions.description: $emit includes connectionName and topic fields

assertions.name: valid_source_with_topic
assertions.description: $source includes connectionName 'my-kafka' and topic 'raw-events'

evals.prompt: I need a processor in workspace dev-streams (project 65094f059e0776665611598b) that reads from my-kafka topic 'orders', does a $lookup to enrich with customer data from my-atlas (db: shop, coll: customers), then writes enriched orders to my-atlas (db: shop, coll: enriched_orders). The processor needs to handle high throughput — about 10,000 events/second with the $lookup. What tier do I need and show me the pipeline.
evals.expected_output: Should recommend SP30+ with parallelism on $lookup and $merge.

assertions.name: recommends_sp30_or_higher
assertions.description: LLM recommends SP30 or SP50 tier, not SP10 or lower

assertions.name: lookup_has_parallelism
assertions.description: $lookup stage includes parallelism setting > 1

assertions.name: merge_has_parallelism
assertions.description: $merge stage includes parallelism setting for concurrent writes

assertions.name: explains_sizing_rationale
assertions.description: LLM explains why the recommended tier is needed

evals.prompt: Delete connection prod-kafka from workspace prod-streams in project 65094f059e0776665611598b. The processor etl-pipeline uses this connection and is currently running.
evals.expected_output: Must warn that deletion will fail because a running processor references this connection.

assertions.name: warns_about_running_processor
assertions.description: LLM warns that the connection cannot be deleted while a running processor references it

assertions.name: suggests_stop_or_delete_processor_first
assertions.description: LLM suggests stopping or deleting the etl-pipeline processor before deleting the connection

assertions.name: does_not_blindly_delete
assertions.description: LLM does NOT immediately call atlas-streams-teardown without addressing the dependency

assertions.name: explains_dependency_mechanism
assertions.description: LLM explains WHY deletion fails — Atlas blocks this to prevent pipeline breakage

evals.prompt: I have a pipeline with $lookup (parallelism: 4), $https (parallelism: 3), and $merge (parallelism: 4). What's the minimum tier I need for this processor?
evals.expected_output: Should calculate (4-1)+(3-1)+(4-1) = 8, recommend SP10.

assertions.name: shows_parallelism_formula
assertions.description: LLM shows the parallelism formula: sum of (parallelism - 1) for each stage

assertions.name: calculates_eight
assertions.description: LLM arrives at 8 as the total excess parallelism needed

assertions.name: recommends_sp10
assertions.description: LLM recommends SP10 as the minimum tier (max parallelism 8)

assertions.name: references_tier_table
assertions.description: LLM references the tier's max parallelism limit to justify the recommendation

evals.prompt: Create a Kafka connection called prod-kafka in workspace dev-streams (project 65094f059e0776665611598b) for my Confluent Cloud cluster.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT ask for passwords/bootstrap servers. Should call build tool and let elicitation handle sensitive fields.

assertions.name: does_not_ask_for_password
assertions.description: LLM does NOT ask the user for their Kafka password or authentication credentials

assertions.name: does_not_ask_for_bootstrap_servers
assertions.description: LLM does NOT ask the user for bootstrap server addresses before calling the tool

assertions.name: calls_build_tool
assertions.description: LLM calls or proposes calling atlas-streams-build with resource connection, connectionType Kafka

assertions.name: trusts_elicitation
assertions.description: LLM mentions or implies that the tool will collect missing fields, or proceeds without gathering sensitive config first

evals.expected_output: Should identify idle partitions blocking window closure, recommend partitionIdleTimeout.

assertions.name: identifies_idle_partitions
assertions.description: LLM identifies idle Kafka partitions as the likely cause — windows require all partitions to advance

assertions.name: recommends_partition_idle_timeout
assertions.description: LLM recommends adding partitionIdleTimeout to the $source stage

assertions.name: shows_corrected_pipeline
assertions.description: LLM shows the corrected sp.process() call with partitionIdleTimeout in $source

assertions.name: notes_sp_process_single_threaded
assertions.description: LLM mentions that sp.process() is single-threaded

evals.prompt: I have a workspace with my-kafka (Kafka) and my-s3 (S3) connections in project 65094f059e0776665611598b. Create a processor that reads from Kafka topic 'logs', and writes all logs to S3 bucket 'data-lake' under the prefix 'raw/logs/' as JSON files. Don't call the build tool, just show me the pipeline JSON.
evals.expected_output: Must use $emit for S3 with 'path' (not 'prefix'), include outputFormat config, mention IAM role ARN prerequisite.

assertions.name: uses_path_not_prefix
assertions.description: S3 $emit uses 'path' field, NOT 'prefix'

assertions.name: includes_output_format
assertions.description: $emit includes config.outputFormat (e.g., {name: 'json'})

assertions.name: mentions_iam_prereq
assertions.description: LLM mentions IAM role ARN must be registered via Atlas Cloud Provider Access

assertions.name: valid_s3_emit_structure
assertions.description: $emit has connectionName, bucket, and path fields

evals.prompt: I have an HTTPS connection called my-api pointing to a REST endpoint. I want to create a processor that uses my-api as the source to read data from the API and write it to my-atlas cluster. Show me the pipeline.
evals.expected_output: Must explain HTTPS cannot be used as $source. Should suggest proper sources (Kafka, Cluster change stream) with HTTPS as mid-pipeline enrichment.

assertions.name: explains_https_not_source
assertions.description: LLM explains that HTTPS connections cannot be used as $source

assertions.name: no_https_in_source
assertions.description: LLM does NOT provide a pipeline with an HTTPS connection in $source

assertions.name: suggests_alternative_source
assertions.description: LLM suggests a valid source type (Kafka, Cluster change stream) instead

assertions.name: explains_https_capabilities
assertions.description: LLM explains what HTTPS connections CAN do (mid-pipeline $https enrichment or as a sink)

evals.prompt: I have a workspace with my-kafka (Kafka), my-atlas (Cluster), and enrichment-api (Https) connections. Create a processor that reads from Kafka topic 'orders', calls the enrichment API at path '/v1/enrich' (POST) to add risk scores, then writes to Atlas db 'shop' collection 'scored_orders'. Show me the pipeline JSON.
evals.expected_output: Must include $https with connectionName, path, method, as, and onError: 'dlq'. API auth in connection settings not pipeline.

assertions.name: https_has_required_fields
assertions.description: $https stage includes connectionName, path, method (POST), and 'as' field

assertions.name: https_has_onerror_dlq
assertions.description: $https stage includes onError: 'dlq' (not 'fail' or missing)

assertions.name: no_hardcoded_auth
assertions.description: LLM does NOT hardcode API keys/auth headers in the pipeline (should be in connection settings)

assertions.name: valid_pipeline_structure
assertions.description: Pipeline has $source (Kafka), $https enrichment, and $merge to Atlas

evals.prompt: I have my-kinesis-in (Kinesis) and my-kinesis-out (Kinesis) connections. Create a processor that reads from Kinesis stream 'clickstream-raw', filters for events where action='purchase', and writes to Kinesis stream 'purchases'. Show me the pipeline JSON.
evals.expected_output: Must use 'stream' (not 'topic') for Kinesis $source and $emit. $emit must include partitionKey. Must mention IAM prereq.

assertions.name: source_uses_stream_not_topic
assertions.description: Kinesis $source uses 'stream' field, NOT 'topic' or 'streamName'

assertions.name: emit_uses_stream_not_topic
assertions.description: Kinesis $emit uses 'stream' field, NOT 'topic'

assertions.name: emit_has_partition_key
assertions.description: $emit includes a 'partitionKey' field (required for Kinesis)

assertions.name: mentions_iam_prereq
assertions.description: LLM mentions IAM role ARN must be registered via Atlas Cloud Provider Access

evals.prompt: I have my-kafka (Kafka), my-atlas (Cluster), and my-lambda (AWSLambda) connections. Create a processor that reads from Kafka topic 'transactions', calls my Lambda function 'fraud-scorer' to get a fraud score for each transaction, then writes scored transactions to Atlas db 'finance' collection 'scored_transactions'. Show me the pipeline.
evals.expected_output: Must use $externalFunction (not $emit) for Lambda. Mid-pipeline can be sync or async. Must include onError: 'dlq'.

assertions.name: uses_external_function_not_emit
assertions.description: Uses $externalFunction for Lambda, NOT $emit

assertions.name: has_required_fields
assertions.description: $externalFunction includes connectionName, functionName, execution, as, and onError

assertions.name: onerror_is_dlq
assertions.description: $externalFunction has onError: 'dlq'

assertions.name: terminal_sink_is_merge
assertions.description: Final stage is $merge to Atlas (Lambda is mid-pipeline, not the terminal sink)

evals.prompt: I want to send all processed events to my Lambda function as the final destination. I have my-kafka (Kafka) and my-lambda (AWSLambda) connections. Create a processor that reads from Kafka topic 'alerts' and sends each alert to Lambda function 'alert-handler'. Show me the pipeline.
evals.expected_output: Must use $externalFunction with execution: 'async' (sync is invalid for sink). Must mention IAM prereq.

assertions.name: uses_external_function_not_emit
assertions.description: Uses $externalFunction as terminal stage, NOT $emit for Lambda

assertions.name: execution_is_async
assertions.description: $externalFunction has execution: 'async' (sync is NOT valid for sink/terminal stage)

assertions.name: explains_async_requirement
assertions.description: LLM explains that $externalFunction as a sink must use async execution

assertions.name: mentions_iam_prereq
assertions.description: LLM mentions IAM role ARN must be registered via Atlas Cloud Provider Access

evals.prompt: I need to set up Avro schema validation for my Kafka connection. I have a Confluent Schema Registry at https://schema-registry.example.com. How do I create the schema registry connection in workspace dev-streams (project 65094f059e0776665611598b)?
evals.expected_output: connectionType must be 'SchemaRegistry' (not 'Kafka'). Must include provider, schemaRegistryUrls, and auth config.

assertions.name: correct_connection_type
assertions.description: Uses connectionType 'SchemaRegistry', NOT 'Kafka'

assertions.name: includes_provider
assertions.description: Config includes provider field (e.g., 'CONFLUENT')

assertions.name: includes_schema_registry_urls
assertions.description: Config includes schemaRegistryUrls with the registry URL

assertions.name: mentions_auth_types
assertions.description: LLM mentions authentication options: USER_INFO (explicit credentials) or SASL_INHERIT (inherit from Kafka connection)

evals.prompt: I need a single processor that reads from Kafka topic 'events', enriches each event with a $lookup to Atlas, then writes the enriched events to BOTH an Atlas collection AND a Kafka topic. Show me the pipeline for workspace dev-streams (project 65094f059e0776665611598b).
evals.expected_output: Must explain single-sink constraint and propose chained processors (two separate pipelines).

assertions.name: explains_single_sink_constraint
assertions.description: LLM explains that a single pipeline can only have ONE terminal sink ($merge or $emit), not both

assertions.name: proposes_chained_processors
assertions.description: LLM proposes two separate processors (chained pattern) to achieve dual output

assertions.name: shows_both_pipelines
assertions.description: LLM shows both processor pipelines — one writing to Atlas, one to Kafka

assertions.name: explains_intermediate
assertions.description: LLM explains the intermediate connection pattern (Kafka-as-intermediate or Atlas-as-intermediate)

evals.prompt: I want to add schema validation to my processor pipeline. If a document doesn't match the expected schema, I want the processor to reject it with an error. Here's what I have so far: [{"$source": {"connectionName": "my-kafka", "topic": "events"}}, {"$merge": {"into": {"connectionName": "my-atlas", "db": "app", "coll": "events"}}}]. Add a $validate stage before the $merge.
evals.expected_output: Must use validationAction: 'dlq' (not 'error'). Error crashes the processor. Must include $jsonSchema.

assertions.name: uses_dlq_not_error
assertions.description: $validate uses validationAction: 'dlq', NOT 'error'

assertions.name: explains_error_crashes
assertions.description: LLM explains that validationAction: 'error' will crash the processor and should not be used

assertions.name: includes_json_schema
assertions.description: $validate includes a validator with $jsonSchema

assertions.name: corrects_user_request
assertions.description: LLM corrects the user's request to 'reject with an error' by explaining why 'dlq' is the safe choice

evals.name: mongosh inspection
evals.prompt: I'm connected to my Atlas Stream Processing workspace via mongosh. My MCP tools aren't available right now. I need to see what connections are configured, which processors are currently running, and get a health snapshot of one of them. What mongosh commands do I use?
evals.expected_output: Must use sp.listConnections(), sp.listStreamProcessors(), and sp.stats(). Must NOT suggest standard MongoDB shell commands like db.listCollections().

assertions.name: uses_list_connections
assertions.description: Uses sp.listConnections() to list configured connections

assertions.name: uses_list_processors
assertions.description: Uses sp.listStreamProcessors() to list deployed processors

assertions.name: mentions_sp_stats
assertions.description: Mentions sp.stats() for processor health and statistics

assertions.name: no_wrong_commands
assertions.description: Does NOT suggest standard MongoDB shell commands (db.listCollections(), show collections, db.getCollectionNames()) that would not work in a streams workspace

evals.name: no-tools pipeline test
evals.prompt: I want to quickly test if my new pipeline logic works — I need to filter Kafka topic 'orders' on connection my-kafka for amount > 500 and see what documents come out. Can you help me run this? Note: the atlas-streams-build tool is not available in this session.
evals.expected_output: Should suggest sp.process() in mongosh as the ephemeral testing path. Should NOT suggest deploying a processor. Must show a sp.process() example with the correct pipeline.

assertions.name: suggests_sp_process
assertions.description: Proactively suggests sp.process() in mongosh as the way to test without deploying

assertions.name: shows_sp_process_example
assertions.description: Shows an actual sp.process() call with $source (my-kafka, topic: orders) and $match (amount > 500)

assertions.name: mentions_ephemeral_free
assertions.description: Mentions sp.process() is ephemeral (no processor deployed) and/or free (no billing)

assertions.name: no_false_tool_call
assertions.description: Does NOT attempt to call atlas-streams-build or atlas-streams-manage as if they exist

evals.name: no-tools config fix
evals.prompt: I just set up the MongoDB MCP server but I don't see any atlas-streams tools in my Claude session. I need to create a connection to my Kafka cluster and deploy a processor. What should I do?
evals.expected_output: Must diagnose previewFeatures: ['streams'] as the missing config. Must show the config fix. Should offer Atlas CLI as interim while user fixes the config.

assertions.name: diagnoses_preview_features
assertions.description: Identifies previewFeatures: ["streams"] as the likely missing MCP server config causing the tools to be absent

assertions.name: shows_config_fix
assertions.description: Shows the specific JSON config change needed to add previewFeatures: ["streams"] to the MCP server settings

assertions.name: offers_cli_interim
assertions.description: Offers Atlas CLI (atlas streams commands) as an interim option while the user fixes the MCP config

assertions.name: no_false_tool_call
assertions.description: Does NOT attempt to call atlas-streams-build or other atlas-streams-* tools as if they exist

evals.name: CLI list processors
evals.prompt: My MCP server isn't connecting. I need to check what processors are running in workspace prod-streams (project 65094f059e0776665611598b) and see the pipeline definition for a specific one. How can I do this without the MCP tools?
evals.expected_output: Must use correct atlas streams processors list --instance syntax. Must use --instance flag (not --workspace). Must show the describe command for pipeline details.

assertions.name: correct_list_command
assertions.description: Uses 'atlas streams processors list' with correct subcommand structure (not 'atlas stream' or wrong verb)

assertions.name: uses_instance_flag
assertions.description: Uses '--instance prod-streams' flag (NOT '--workspace' or other flag name)

assertions.name: includes_project_id
assertions.description: Includes '--projectId 65094f059e0776665611598b' in the commands

assertions.name: shows_describe_command
assertions.description: Shows 'atlas streams processors describe <name> --instance prod-streams' for getting pipeline details
